Alpha Weapons.name: Tonans (vs Titan)
Alpha Weapons.notes:
VERY IMPORTANT: While Tonans beats Vendicatoire in terms of burst vs Titans at base, the Titan damage boost is additive with boosts from nuclear amplifier, etc
TLDR: Vendicatoire does ~15% more damage than Tonans when both have a fully stacked NA on a damage dealer titan.

Alpha Weapons.name: Vendicatoire
Alpha Weapons.notes: Burst DPS for weapons that unload in under 1 second is simply replaced with damage per burst to avoid confusion.

Alpha Weapons.name: Anguisher (25m)
Alpha Weapons.notes: Includes DoT, much of which is done while reloading

Alpha Weapons.name: Void (25m)
Alpha Weapons.notes: The suppression after reloading does like 1k damage per tick lmao, not accounted for

Alpha Weapons.name: Furnace (Heat x1)
Alpha Weapons.notes: This is the damage dealt by a single furnace shooting at a target that can accumulate heat. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Alpha Weapons.name: Furnace (Heat x2)
Alpha Weapons.notes: This is the damage dealt by each furnace when you have two furnaces, torches, or a combination of the two. Yes, they do less damage the more you have. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Alpha Weapons.name: Furnace (Heat x3)
Alpha Weapons.notes: This is the damage dealt by each furnace when you have three furnaces, torches, or a combination of the three. Yes, they do less damage the more you have. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Alpha Weapons.name: UE Grom (25m)
Alpha Weapons.notes: I didnt bother calculating the cycle DPS because it will never be relevant. It takes almost 3 straight minutes of shooting without stopping to run out of ammo.

Alpha Weapons.name: Striker
Alpha Weapons.notes: Burst DPS for weapons that unload in under 1 second is simply replaced with damage per burst to avoid confusion.

Alpha Weapons.name: Basilisk
Alpha Weapons.notes: Includes DoT damage, much of which is done while reloading.

Alpha Weapons.name: Arbiter (boosted)
Alpha Weapons.notes: Assumes you fire it properly (starts spun up, doesnt overheat)

Alpha Weapons.name: Furnace (No Heat)
Alpha Weapons.notes: Assumes you're shooting a shield, which cannot accumulate heat. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Alpha Weapons.name: Arbiter (no boost)
Alpha Weapons.notes: Assumes you fire it properly (starts spun up, doesnt overheat)

Beta Weapons.name: Fulger (vs Titan)
Beta Weapons.notes:
VERY IMPORTANT: While titan Zeus weapons show good performance vs Titans at base, the Titan damage boost is additive with boosts from nuclear amplifier, etc. 
TLDR: Vendicatoire does ~15% more damage than Tonans when both have a fully stacked NA on a damage dealer titan.

Beta Weapons.notes: This is the damage dealt by a single torch shooting at a target that can accumulate heat. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Beta Weapons.name: Torch (Heat x2)

Beta Weapons.name: Torch (Heat x3)
Beta Weapons.notes: This is the damage dealt by each torch when you have three furnaces, torches, or a combination of the three. Yes, they do less damage the more you have.  CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Beta Weapons.name: Krait
Beta Weapons.notes: Includes DoT damage, much of which is done while reloading.

Beta Weapons.name: Equalizer (boosted)
Beta Weapons.notes: Assumes you fire it properly (starts spun up, doesnt overheat)

Beta Weapons.name: Torch (No Heat)

Beta Weapons.name: Equalizer (no boost)

Heavy Weapons.name: Liodari (Charged)
Heavy Weapons.notes:
A full charge requires enemies to deal about 350k damage to you. Shields count.
Burst DPS for weapons that unload in under 1 second is simply replaced with damage per burst to avoid confusion. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Heavy Weapons.name: Liodari (Uncharged)
Heavy Weapons.notes: Burst DPS for weapons that unload in under 1 second is simply replaced with damage per burst to avoid confusion. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Heavy Weapons.name: Basileus
Heavy Weapons.notes: Fragility effect can increase damage against enemies with defense points

Heavy Weapons.name: UE Incinerator
Heavy Weapons.notes: CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL. UE Incinerator gets about 90% of a blast per mag, so 90% of a blast is accounted for in the DPS.

Heavy Weapons.name: Kelvin
Heavy Weapons.notes: Assumes you stop shooting before they overheat, so the cooldown is only 5 seconds rather than 6 seconds. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Heavy Weapons.name: Hammer (25m)
Heavy Weapons.notes: One Hammer can get about half a blast per mag, so half a blast is accounted for in the DPS (assuming you're using 2+ Hammer)

Heavy Weapons.name: Pshent
Heavy Weapons.notes: If you start the fight with 2 orbitals prepared, your burst DPS will be double, but only for that initial burst.

Heavy Weapons.name: Boom
Heavy Weapons.notes: Burst DPS for weapons that unload in under 1 second is simply replaced with damage per burst to avoid confusion. NO DEATHMARK BONUS

Heavy Weapons.name: Pilum
Heavy Weapons.notes: Pilum gets one third of a blast per mag, so one third of a blast is accounted for in the DPS.

Heavy Weapons.name: Piercer (Max Fatigue)
Heavy Weapons.notes: Assumes you're already at max fatigue stacks (5 stacks)

Heavy Weapons.name: Leinani (Machine Gun)
Heavy Weapons.notes: Includes the DoT damage, much of which is dealt while reloading.

Heavy Weapons.name: Screamer
Heavy Weapons.notes: Each sonicblast does 25k damage. A full mag of 1 screamer results in 1 blast. The blast is accounted for in the DPS. Spread at range not accounted for.

Heavy Weapons.name: Hornet
Heavy Weapons.notes: Includes DoT damage, much of which is done while reloading

Heavy Weapons.name: Nucleon (25m)
Heavy Weapons.notes: At close range, you dont need to let them cool off, so they can continually do their burst dps forever

Heavy Weapons.name: Nucleon (500m)
Heavy Weapons.notes: At longer range, nucleon needs to cool off about 75% of the way to max heat in order to hit every shot

Heavy Weapons.name: Cestus
Heavy Weapons.notes: One Cestus can get about half a blast per mag, so half a blast is accounted for in the DPS (assuming you're using 2+ Cestus)

Heavy Weapons.name: UE Dragoon
Heavy Weapons.notes: 31 second unload time lmao, you probably won't need to worry about the cycle dps

Heavy Weapons.name: Talon
Heavy Weapons.notes: Fragility effect can increase damage against enemies with defense points

Heavy Weapons.name: Leinani (Energy Blaster)
Heavy Weapons.notes: Charges ships slowly, but concentrates damage in bursty energy balls that have small splash.

Heavy Weapons.name: Fengbao
Heavy Weapons.notes: Let's be honest, nobody using this weapon is using it because it does a ton of damage.

Medium Weapons.name: Kroko (Charged)

A full charge requires enemies to deal about 350k damage to you. Shields count. 
Burst DPS for weapons that unload in under 1 second is simply replaced with damage per burst to avoid confusion. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Medium Weapons.name: Murix (25m)
Medium Weapons.notes: Includes DoT damage, much of which is dealt while reloading. You'll only get this much damage at point blank.

Medium Weapons.name: UE Arm M (Otto, High % DMG)
Medium Weapons.notes: Assumes you're shooting a bot with 1M HP on average (% damage is based on current HP, not max HP) Keep in mind % damage cannot be mitigated by defense points.

Medium Weapons.name: Kroko (Uncharged)
Medium Weapons.notes: Burst DPS for weapons that unload in under 1 second is simply replaced with damage per burst to avoid confusion. CURRENTLY INFLATED BY INTEL.

Medium Weapons.name: UE Cryo
Medium Weapons.notes: Range is 350m but projectile travel time signficiantly lowers damage output so point blank is assumed. Cryo weapons rarely actually hit their full DPS potential. Freeze damage boost accounted for.

Medium Weapons.name: UE Havoc (25m)
Medium Weapons.notes: 100% grey damage is pretty badass

Medium Weapons.name: UE Arm M (High % DMG)
Medium Weapons.notes: Assumes you're shooting a bot with 1M HP on average (% damage is based on current HP, not max HP) Keep in mind % damage cannot be mitigated by defense points.

Medium Weapons.name: UE Corona (150m)
